reviews.review_text:
After hours check in was a breeze. Unfortunately, it rained most of the weekend. However Georgia from reception made it very easy to upgrade to a concrete slab, which honestly saved the weekend.
Pool are was amazing! If the weather was better, we would have been in there all weekend. Would be good if the spa bubbles area was heated. But that’s not a deal breaker. Big cheese pieces could use a bit of glue so the bits don’t fall off but we could still play.
We will 100% be back when its sunny!
Very underwhelming when you arrive, nothing like the photos and kids are a little disappointed because of it. Small water park is good, no coffee machine on site, good size camping spots.

We camped there on a powered site, close to most destinations.
Clean facilities, hot water, quiet after 10pm.
Kids loved the jumping pillow and the pool.
The guys here are so accommodating! I bough my two children here and it was such a relaxed stay. The pool and jumping pillow are awesome!

Powered camp ground was exactly as it said it was: a powered site.

The closing the jumping pillow at 6 is poor form, (and for some reason 5pm on Easter Sunday)... and the guy closing it was extremely rude to the kids looking for something to do.
Nice grounds, good facilities and clean

Don't book here if there maybe a chance you need to cancel your booking for any reason. They keep your first night deposit  and will not refund.
All other Holiday parks other then this one refund or give credit which is fair. Or a small cancelation fee. Not $114 .
So avoid booking this Holiday park if you want to throw away your deposit.

We were due to come late and advised reception when booking, asking if we could be placed away from other campers because of this & having 2 young children.
We were placed directly in between 2 families while there was tonnes of sights elsewhere that stayed vacant our entire stay.
There was one toilet for hoards of people which was almost nearly always in use when needed.
Amenities were not very well taken care of and very dated.
Pool was freezing and spa type area appeared to be broken.
Overall the stay was ok but wouldn't be back, especially due to price being very similar to much nicer caravan parks nearby.

I will never book my van in again  to many dogs with owners that don't know how or won't keep them until control
Way too many dogs in the park

Es ist ein sehr schöner Caravanplatz. Die Sanitäranlagen sind sauber und gepflegt. Die Poollandschaft ist sehr schön. Das Wasser ist sauber und die große Rutsche ist das highlight überhaupt bei groß und klein.|Die Plätze verfügen über genug Abstand, Privatsphäre ist gegeben. |Die Waschmaschinen haben wir nicht genutzt, da hatten wir schon bessere, Wäscheleine war wenig vorhanden und die vorhandene war mehrmals verknotet, das hat mich persönlich nicht überzeugt. Hier habe ich Hoffnung dass ich bei dem nächsten Platz besseres finde.|Trotzdem gibt es eine 100 % Weiterempfehlung.
Many different activities in the park for kids and adults.  Facilities so clean, just didn't like being camper right next to them nor the dump point.

Great experience, great service, loved how clean the park is .
Brilliant out door area for the kids.
Excellent time

Great family holiday park with hidden gems (kids friendly pool with a slide, caravans converted into accomodation giving a u pique feel). The park’s location is close to major activity/amenities area.

The check in was smooth and the staff was friendly and patient in explaining/check in process.

Definitely see us coming over again.
Neat and clean rooms, kids loved the pool and slide, really nice service

Loved the stay, certainly will stay again.
Very friendly staff, clean facilities, good size pool area which is cleaned daily with a large slide big enough for adults to go down to.

Not a bad park however needs a big injection of funds to bring it up to scratch. Cabin we stayed in was ok and clean had to clean the air-conditioning filters as they were thick as carpet and probably never cleaned . We had stayed in a lot of parks prior to this park and unfortunately dosent compare to others we stayed at. Not much for kids to do except ride your bike or swim in the fairly ordinary sized pool. Met a lot of lovely people but unfortunately this will be our last visit here.

We recently stayed at BIG4 Bays Holiday Park on sites 24 and 25. We had booked well in advance and were advised that our site number would only be allocated on the day of arrival, which didn’t leave us with much choice in location. Unfortunately, we were placed on a grass site without a concrete slab, even though neighbouring sites had one, which was a bit disappointing.

We were also informed prior to arrival that dogs were not permitted, however during our stay we saw at least five dogs in the park, which was confusing and frustrating given the strict advice we received.

The amenities block could have been maintained better. There was quite a bit of dirt and sand across the floors by the evening. The pool area was nice and the kids enjoyed it, although it is on the smaller side, especially during busy periods.

WiFi was another issue, as it wasn’t working for most of our stay. This meant the kids had to hotspot from my phone during downtime. The park is also quite close to the main road, and we could hear cars and motorbikes passing, particularly at night.

Laundry facilities were available, but at $6 per wash and $6 per dry, it felt quite expensive.

Overall, while there were some positives, we didn’t feel the stay represented good value for the cost per night, and unfortunately we would be unlikely to return.

Was a very nice stay ay Bays Holiday Park. I read mixed reviews but it really was a pleasant stay. Facilities are in reasonable condition and there was plenty of them. Well appointed park. Lots of activities for kids. Friendly and helpful staff. We even moved our arrival date by two days and it was no hassle at all.

We stayed here recently and overall the experience was mixed, with significant issues around booking and site allocation.

Before booking, I had called to enquire if they could accommodate sites for our group of families, grandparents - some in tents and some in cabins.
They confirmed yes, gave instructions to put a name in comments section and everyone proceeded to book their own site accordingly.
Four days before arrival, when I contacted to confirm if our tent sites were located close together and the final family to make a booking, the park said they had only just realised we were a group booking, despite all bookings quoting my name in the comments as instructed.
They then said they don’t accept group bookings at this time of year. And refused to accept the final family - even though there was availability advertised online.
This led to unnecessary stress right before our holiday.

Two days before arrival, they reversed their position and allowed our final family to attend, which we appreciated - however, even on check-in our not all our camp sites were located close together, despite being told this was the reason for booking as a group.

The camping sites themselves were powered but not ideal for tents, located along a dusty gravel road. This meant dirt and dust constantly through the tent, and it wasn’t great for young kids riding scooters. We were also placed near a dump bin, which affected the overall feel of the site.

Facilities were mixed. Bathrooms were okay and clean enough. The pool area looks nice but is quite small for the size of the park, so we often headed out during the day. The kids did enjoy the jumping pillow, volleyball court and soccer field.

There were guests checking in and out throughout our stay, so it was surprising that accommodating our group and placing us closer together was such a challenge.

Not a terrible stay, but not the relaxing family camping experience we had hoped for.

We had a mixed experience of this Holiday Park.

The Highlight would be the pool area with the slide and the jumping pillow for sure! The children had a great time with these facilities.

The room we stayed in was very nice, it included a spa bath. However, most of the cabin had carpet running through it which was evidently quite dusty and has not had a proper clean in a long time. After a couple days the floors/carpet became quite dirty due to having two toddlers with us. There was a broom provided in the room to sweep, however this wasn’t sufficient to clean the carpet. We asked reception for a vacuum to use and they advised they are unable to provide us with one, however would ‘try’ get a cleaner to come and do it for us.
This did not happen and led us to leaving 2 nights early.

The wifi/internet reception is not good. We could barely make calls with our phones and the tvs are old school and small. This is not a necessity to have, however would have been nice to use applications like Netflix at night time to wind down with the kids.

Another thing to note the park is located off a main road with no walking tracks around. You need to drive approximately 8mins to reach some local shops. Again this is not a necessity but we do enjoy going for walks on holidays and thought it would be good to note for families who do enjoy going for walks. You are able to walk around the camping ground, however not pram friendly due to rocky road/grass.

Overall we still enjoyed our stay.

We’ve been coming to the same place for two years now and everything has been great. The location, clean rooms, friendly service, and how suitable it is for families with children really stand out. There are also lots of activities nearby. The pool, playground, and games make it even better. Definitely recommended 👍

Very expensive. The grounds keeper was very rude. My child was scared going down a slide so I was going down with him. The ground keeper abused me. This was after older kids were blocking the slide a going down with their boggy boards.

Very run down facilities. Kids facilities not working. A/c was on its last legs.

Never

Mixed review. Good location. Outdated main bathroom. Stayed in a non busy period, bathrooms were dirty and ran out of hot water on a Monday.
Fly and mozzie central!

Pool and jumping pillow are presented well and clean.
More slab sites around would be ideal as we stayed next to the jumping pillow and was quite sandy and full of bindi
Not what the photos show space for van barely fits our site. I will be getting a better park next time
What a great park for families and at an awesome price that people can actually afford have had the best time here that we have actually come back 2 weekends in a row
Easy to book, accommodated 4 other families to ensure we were in close proximity, heaps to do for the kids. A wonderful environment for our group to enjoy a memorable weekend

Had to leave early as had an emergency family issue no refund or even change of date was offered  .  The place if full of bindi’s flys / mosquitoes that bite and leave marks all over  would not recommend this place for kids
Easy to get to everything in Nelson Bay & surrounds. Great amenities. Friendly staff.

Group of 4 families, from tent, camper trailer, van and Cabin. Great weekend for 3 generations. Thank you to Steve for helping us move Cabins so that we could be closer to the kids and grand kids, from 200 mtrs to 2 mtrs. So many activities, resort pool with Slide (would be better heated), play grounds, 10 pin bowling,  hookie, giant chess, soccer,  basketball hoop, volleyball, bird Avery, Pendle cart/bikes.  ..... forgot something,  Kmart, Woolworths and Coles, short drive away, Bunnings even closer. ...... we all enjoyed the weekend,  gave all of us a time to pause and be together, thank you again to Steve and staff.

Had a great stay.

Camp was great and reception was also very nice.

Would go back.

amazing stay experience! adorable trailer, beautiful scenery and a super fun waterslide :) will definitely be back soon! thank you big 4 for helping us have a nice relaxing weekend away from the real world <3
Bays treated us like royalty. Steve helped group our family sites together. Nothing was a problem. Highly recommended 👌

Thanks BIG4 Bays Holiday Park for a fabulous stay… great location and facilities… even my 4 legged friends had a terrific time.
I also just wanted to say a special thankyou to Georgia ( at reception) you truly are an asset to the business.
Until next time
MelJB
Great location, friendly staff and nice accommodation. Would have been great to have a bbq but we did have a spa bath in the cabin.

We loved our stay at Bays Holiday Park! The kids loved the pool and jumping pillow.
The park was very conveniently located close to local a local horse riding centre and the sting ray and shark facility, which gave us loads to entertain the kids with. The staff were warm and friendly.
We staying in Matilda the Cosy Caravan and it was perfect for a family - much bigger than it looks in the photos. We will definitely be back.

Nice park. We were there in winter.  Near empty.. 6 min from the beach and 8 min from major shopping centre.
We had a lot of rain. Some sites did flood, particularly around the pool area. Ground is sand so most sites with pads do drain well.
Busy road runs past entry. Normal suburban road noise in the morning  through to dark.
Activities for the kids.  Big camp kitchen but no stove or cooktop. Toilets and showers are aged but very clean. Modern pool area.
Would be happy to stay again.

BIG4 Port Stephens is truly a unique place. We stayed for two nights and our experience was very special. The location is excellent—close to beaches, shops, and plenty of attractions for all ages. The park itself is peaceful and well-maintained, with friendly staff and great facilities. It’s perfect for families, couples, or anyone looking to relax and explore the beauty of Port Stephens. We especially appreciated how easy it was to get around and discover nearby spots. Highly recommended for a memorable getaway!

Had a great stay! In a perfect location, family friendly environment, loved staying here! The facilities are clean and everything is well maintained. Thanks to Georgia at reception for her friendly and helpful service. Highly recommend
nice campervan site, highly recommended

A very big thankyou to Lynn and Georgia both for there welcome and accommodating to my changing situation and Charli girl 💖 and especially to Georgia who supplied me with some food to keep me going till the rain and winds subsided enough for me to get supplies. So truely grateful 💖
The cabin I have been staying in has been delightful, with its calming decor and everything you need microwave, TV,  bbq, crockery and cutlery. A lovely closed in balcony out the front was perfect for BC Charli girl  to play sleep and eat. The ground and facilities are amazing enough to keep young and old entertained for hours all surrounded by beautiful well established gym trees. Thankyou x

Huge thanks to Georgia and Lynn — truly wonderful hosts who made my stay so welcoming and easy. Friendly, helpful, and always ready to assist with a smile. Their hospitality really stood out and made all the difference! 🙌

The shower block was spotless — clean and fresh, which is a total win when you’re camping. 🚿✨

Stayed during winter and wet weather, so the campsite was nice and quiet, perfect for some peace and calm. 🌧️🤫

Great location too — right on the main road, close to everything you need without the noise. 🛣️🛒

If you want a place with friendly faces and a comfy vibe, Big4 Bays Holiday Park is where it’s at! 🌈⛺

We had a lovely stay on a level concrete site for our camper. Quiet, friendly help. Facilities were clean, The area is lovely, so many beautiful bays close by! Georgia was especially helpful, with tips and her knowledge of the area, not inky here but also where we want to go next - thanks!
Gut, wie (fast) jeder Big4... Leider nicht die beste Lage

My first Caravan Park experience with a my first van! Get location, the young girl in reception was lovely, excellent customer service, pleasant and helpful, the park itself is well set up and all services an amenities easily accessible and well functioning. Great first experience, I will definitely be back! 👌

False advertising, for $147 a night for a powered site, I expected a high end caravan park, no boom gate for security, no concrete slabs on the site, bindis everywhere, roads are full of pot holes, dogs off leads, pool looks nothing like the photos, no phone reception, WiFi doesn't work, service was terrible we were told if we don't like it we can leave, which we did, DO NOT RECOMMEND
Nice 'little' Big4 park - 2 nights camping - all good

The kids had lots of fun on the entertainment facilities provided. Our unpowered site was very shaded, which was very challenging for solar power, but would have been great for keeping cool on hot days. We were overrun with mossies, which discouraged us from spending any more time on our site than absolutely necessary. As a result, we used the camp kitchen and BBQs a lot, which were good, except for the lack of stove top facilities.
Nice grassy sites. 3 amenity blocks. Beaut pool. Further from the bays/beaches than we thought.

Fantastic place!!|We were there from the USA with family.|We stayed in a cabin and it was very clean and comfortable.  Had everything we needed and more.|When we are in Australia again we will be staying there again.|Great pool and close to Anna Bay.|Thank you

Save your money & go somewhere else.$$$Way to expensive for a camp site that has no cement, long grass & Green Ant's. One of our family member's could Not come & no refund or credit given. Pool OK but way to small for all of the kid's that were there & not safe at all. People partying till the wee morning hour's & No security at all. 😫

Great for families and groups! We enjoyed a fun-filled weekend but best of all, the size and layout of the park made it an easy weekend, which is so important with kids. The grounds are lush and so well maintained, the pool is fun with something for everyone. Upon reading previous negative reviews we were nervous as to what to expect but we were blown away with the park facilities and cleanliness!! Would come back!

We stayed in the tent and for this price it’s good I’d say.
However, I’d never pay the price they ask for a cabin…
Everything is quite dated (showers, kitchen, no kitchen stove as advertised on their website) cockroaches everywhere, and seems like dead animal in the rubbish bin, smells awful …
No one to look after place after hours. Suppose to be family friendly places, people are drinking and being very loud past midnight and no one to ask them to keep it quiet. When with little kids you need this sleep so badly, so not restful at all.
If not noise and awful smell I’d rate our stay on camping grounds 4 stars.
It was a very relaxing couple of days in our van - just what we needed 👍

After reading many mixed reviews online, we were unsure whether to go with this holiday park. We decided to give them a go, and for so many reasons we are glad we did!
This holiday park was the only one in the area that would allow us to stay just one night, rather than their minimum of two. We camped at a non powered site and our site was beautiful. It was private, among the trees, tidy and well maintained. The girls at reception were absolutely lovely and very accommodating to us.
We don’t have kids ourselves, but think this would be a great place to bring a family. The pool area is great, there is SO much space for bikes/scooters etc and the camp kitchen is one of the best we have seen - it has everything and is quite pretty of a night once the festoon lights come on.
The only negative about our stay was that we wished to stay right up the back in the ‘bush camping’ area, but were told that this is not currently available. When booking online this is the image you see and it can be a little deceiving when arriving and finding out you can’t camp there. However, we camped right near it and facing it. So was still very nice.
Overall, we were really happy with our quick stay and will be returning!
Thanks!

Park is good but not for the cost you are charged. I booked a 2 bedroom spa cabin. For two nights it was $710 in total. It was not worth that. We took the kids to the bouncy pad and noticed the gate was locked. We enquired at reception and they said it was sprinkling early so couldnt be used for safety reasons (which is fair). However the next day we went there at 10am it was a hot sunny day and its meant to open at 8am. It was not at all wet. We popped the kids over the fence as it was locked. The manager came by and asked if we popped them over and we said yes and he said you cannot do that. I politely said but its meant to open at 8am its now 10.30am.. it wasnt wet at all or dangerous. With limited facilities for the kids this was really frustrating. Pool was however great for kids. Small but good. There is no reception even with there wifi code. There were cochroaches throughout the cabin. Bindies and green ants everywhere. Id happily stay for a fraction of the cost but for what we paid i expected more.

Not impressed at all. We booked a few cabins here for a weekend away. Very expensive considering location and amenities. The park is small and run down. Our fridge wasn’t working on arrival. The maintenance guy tried to find us a solution as best he could however an old rusty, and mouldy bar fridge placed on the kitchen floor of a very small cabin wasn’t the best solution ( it was the only solution he could provide at the time and they did get a new fridge on the day we were leaving). It was stinking hot so we turned on our air conditioner and it flooded the lounge room of the cabin. It was also fixed but is an inconvenience and indicated a lack of maintenance. Cabin sleeps 6 people, you get a 2x seater lounge. The main bed was a small double bed, very uncomfortable for 2 people. With a noisy fan in the room making intermittent whirling noises and the bushes scraping along the windows in the middle of the night with the slightest breeze meant I didn’t get to sleep until 2am and that was because I was totally exhausted and ended up on the lounge.  The pool is small in comparison to the deceptive photos on the website. It’s the peak of summer and the pool opens at 10am and closes at 7pm, while the sun is still up and it is hot. I watched so many kids come to the pool in the morning to swim and walk away disappointed. It is located in the middle of nowhere, you can’t walk to a beach or anywhere like that. Going anywhere requires you leave the park in a car and drive there. Only one of our cabins had a bbq. There is 1x bbq at the communal kitchen for the entire park to use. I’ve been to many holiday parks and this was far from impressive, particularly when considering the exorbitant  prices they charge. I wouldn’t recommend the place and would give 1 star if it wasn’t for the fact the staff were courteous and did their best to accomodate us.

Booked a weekend away for two nights, but we only end up staying one night. The facility was filthy cockroaches and dirty bedsheets and towels. The fridge was broken so we couldn’t keep our food cool. The only redeeming features of the place was the pool. Services was not the best and not really welcoming

Disappointed in our stay. Value for money was poor for the facilities provided. Pool photos were deceiving, minimal outdated toilets and showers - which in the peak season made them dirtier so quickly. No drainage for caravan, not even grass or concrete slabs for sites. There were so many bindies so not even having a slab was a pain with the kids especially. Pedal carts weren’t available to hire as they don’t operate in the peak season kids were told - even though advertised. Pool was shut for a day because of a kid contaminating the water - there is no other water option for kids. Peak of summer, and at $190 a night being the most expensive Big4 park I’ve stayed at, I would hope there was a back up option.
We did try to speak to staff about our concerns at our stay which were dismissed, I tried to leave a night early and explained my dissapointment requesting a refund for a night, this was denied. I did send a follow up email with my feedback which hasn’t been responded to or acknowledged and it’s been a month since our stay.

My biggest concern was value for money. If I spent $100/$120 per night in peak season I would understand, but at the rate we paid I would at least expect boom gates at the park for security reasons, which we did see the police in the park at one point.

Pros:|Bush setting|Good park for families - had quite alot of outdoor games etc to play|It was available |It was quiet |Pet friendly |Had a slide in the pool||Cons|$450 for 3 nights in an unpowered site|Several conversations with other campers there and we all were charged different prices.|That's off Richter scale. |Really disappointed that people charge these prices, and moreso think it's ok to charge one site $250, the other $450 etc|Come on, it was unpowered......|5 toilets and 5 showers for the whole park|No water hookup at the site, only in the camp kitchen|You had to drive to get to anything around the area|The bathroom door was so hard to open- I had to ask for help off people majority of the time|I spent most of my showering adjusting the temperature |Pool was so small|We definitely wouldn't recommend nor be going back - mainly due to finding out the price difference 😔

Old out dated dirty bathrooms dumped piont was dodgy and stunk payed top dollar in the middle of a swamp wouldn't recommend, the price for this place I have stayed at great locations there is no location here to ride walk anywhere too.

We stayed in the new bunk houses they were absolutely perfect for our needs had to use the amenities which were very clean the park was very neat and tidy.The grounds man Steve was very helpful to our group and help us settle in nothing was a problem definitely would recommend this park

We had a great family camping trip to Bays Big 4 Holiday park.  The site was great, the facilities clean and tidy, and the staff were excellent.  Special mention to Steve who had helped my wife jump start her car as the battery had died.  Much appreciated!
Stayed over Christmas. Great company, great people.

The park is extremely run down. We stayed in a studio cabin. The cabin was not cleaned. The floor was filthy and there were lipstick residue on both the wine glasses and the coffee mugs left from previous guests. The reception hours are not practical, 9.00am - 5.00pm. No staff were monitoring the park and there was no security, anyone could just drive in at any time. Bays Holiday Park is absolutely NOT up to the Big 4 standards. Photos on website are deceiving and not a true reflection of the park at all. Paid for 2 nights but only stayed the one, we certainly were not staying another night.

Avoid at all costs I repeat avoid at all costs especially if u are a family  with females  or young children. My partner a female was haveing a shower in the evening around 11pm and a ADULT MALE hanging around female showers once seeing her enter the shower block he then  eneterd the female shower whilst my female family member was changing . he only walked out once she sreamed loudly and he was then confronted by a male family member wear he continued to say he was a cleaner and very sorry but he was not a cleaner at all but a visiter camping on the far opposite side of the park he had gloves on as well and our female family member was extremely traumatized and still is police wear called and did nothing at all saying lack of evidence due to zero security cameras around the park at all .the  manger a female thought it was pretty much a joke laughing at stages of reporting the incident we had to leave and 2 more family's followed due to females feeling unsafe. The amenities are old and falling to pieces the pool is tiny on a hot day your cramed in like sardines. After all this the manger female  who was  extremely rude would not refund our money  as we couldn't stay thear as the children and females in the family wear traumatized. All the cleaners and grounds keepers look like they hate life and don't want to be thear. Avoid this hell hole at all costs especially family's  I repeat avoid at all costs it's un safe and dangerous thank God my partner got to the guy in the female amenities befor he could hurt anyone. Absolutely zero security on this huge park very unsafe this place needs to be demolished!!!!
I love it because we gets to stay in the cabin and go to the swimming pool 🏊‍♀️

Was very disappointed in this place! The photos are very deceiving! The place needs maintenance and doesn't look like it has had any for a very long time. The office is closed from 11:30-12pm so if you get there for check in just before you're made to wait until all the staff are back from their break. This was not communicated either. The people who arrived before us paid for early check in and got there at 11:30 and was made to wait. For a BIG 4, I expected better. We paid $190 per night for a powered site which was grass and dirt. The roads are gravel so no good for kids with scooters. The toilets have signs they are cleaned a few times a day but the showers were filthy when we went in. There is only 6 showers in the whole park. The bare minimum was done cleaning. The pool was dirty and merky. There was hair and debris floating in it the first day. It was closed the second day early for "maintenance" but the following day, water was still merky. It is not up to big 4 standards. There is also no boom gate so security isn't great. When we complained to management about the toilets and showers being filthy they said "that's unfortunate. I would never stay here again!

Thank you so much for the stay, customer service was amazing and the way the staff shuffled things around for our large group to stay last minute means the world to us!
Thanks again!

Good enough for me for one night in our campervan. Bathrooms are locked with a code, I liked that - feels safer. I didn’t use any other amenities (kitchen, pool or otherwise), but its location is close enough to Nelson Bay, Birubi Beach (Camel Rides & Surfing), Shoal Bay and beyond.
Had a great time, plenty to do and the surrounding area is perfect
Beautiful place, well maintained.

Lovely staff, great location
Internet is very slow

Where do I start.
It doesn't look like the images online..
Very run down. Bindies everywhere so you cannot walk barefoot anywhere, Even in the pool area. The bouncy pillow wasn't inflated when we arrived. And was only inflated late the next day and was only inflated for a short period of time our whole weekend. The room was SO SO hot when we arrived that we had to sit in the car for 2 hours for the whole room to cool down as it was very hot that day. It was raining so we couldn't do anything. The aircon should have been turned on prior to guest arrival time to prevent that happening on extreemly hot days (This was the Matilda carvan cabin) no where to hang wet towels etc, ants everywhere. Not enough bath towels and the beds.... oh my goodness. I had a bad back for 1 week after staying there for 4 nights. They're very firm and uncomfortable. The bunk bed feels flimsy and rocky so my kids didn't want to sleep on the top. It's also got no privacy at night time. You sit in the main room, and if you have a light on, everyone can see inside the whole room. Which was a bit freaky to know that people can just see everything you're doing. Even if you're just sitting there relaxing. Not very comfortable.
The Matilda caravan cabin is right next to the main road!!! We woke up to so much traffic noises on the weekdays, and trucks! I was very disappointed to pay so much money for the Matilda cabin for only 4 nights. $1300 is way too much for what we experienced. I would have been happier to bring my own caravan and pay $200. Way too over priced. No real place for kids to ride their scooters  and if you bring your dogs, please be really careful of the bindies! The bindies were getting stuck to our shoes and I saw so many people's dogs limping around because of this too. The only good thing about this place really is the staff were friendly, and the pool is amazing. My kids really enjoyed the pool  and that will be the only reason we would come back. But I will bring my own caravan and park next to the poolside. The cabins are too over priced for this place and I do not recommend it at all unless your happy to pay thousands for bindie infested grass and a really run down looking place. Sorry but this is a very honest and real review.

My family had the best time staying here. We stayed in the 3 bedroom poolside cabin and had ample space for our family of 5. Our cabin had two separate bathrooms so It was great not having to share a bathroom with the kids! Our cabin even had a full kitchen and dishwasher so less time cleaning up and more time spent by the pool and waterslide, which was a hit for all three kids. My older two had fun playing volleyball with some other kids they met in the park. Grace in the office was super helpful and suggested a sand boarding tour and some local cafes. She went above and beyond and even remembered the kids’ names when we came back to check- out. This is our second time staying and we already can’t wait to come back!
So good

Definitely not up to standards of a big four park. Pool is half the sized in pictures , kids amenities is run down, 1 ping pong  table for a park full of people is ridiculous. One of the  Grounds keeper is rude and arrogant , they shut the pool and pillow way to early . We have pool side cabin and $3800 for seven nights is not worth the price at all . Does not exceed expectations of a big four. Parks on the premises are scabby and run down low grounds maintained , cob webs etc.  this place is not worth the value for the money . Our cabin had a 3 seater lounge for a family of 7/8  , reception wifi lags . Will never book this place again.  Office opens too late and closes too early . Kids trikes are available in the peak heat of the day .
Great place to stay especially with the family and kids. They loved it and all the friends they made. Will definitely be going there again and recommend to all my family and friends.
Stayed for a week in my own van. Great Staff, there's multiple activities and very clean amenities. I'll be staying again when I'm passing thru 👍

I had a very disappointing experience with this hotel. I tried to cancel my booking due to unforeseen circumstances, but the staff refused to accommodate my request. To make matters worse, they were rude during the interaction. I do not recommend this place and will definitely not be booking here again.

We stayed 4 nights in our caravan. The site was half sand half bindii (with no slab).  For a pet friendly caravan park you can't walk your dog or let your dog outside on any grass due to hundreds of bindii. For the price to stay,  the maintenance of the park grounds should be alot better.

This place is honestly a catfish. It doesn’t look the same as the photos they post. It needs a major face lift, we booked two nights and didn’t even stay the second. They also delete negative comments on their socials about the catfish. Really doesn’t meet the standard of the other Big4s. Also $6 for washing is ridiculous.
Fabulous!
It's a dangerous place and you never know what will fall on your head. Fortunately, it didn't hit my child.

First impression upon arriving at the park was that it was very run down and lacked atmosphere. The grounds are not well kept and it looks very untidy.

We booked a deluxe spa cabin and although it was clean, the overwhelming smell of Ajax spray and wipe throughout the cabin is very offensive. Would definitely recommend switching to a fragrance free cleaning product in future. Beautiful outlook to the trees but is interrupted with a giant shed and caravan holding area and some random taped off section that makes it feel like we’re staying in a construction site. Shame cause there’s so much potential but just seems like the owners have let it go.

The heater / air con is extremely loud and randomly is situated in the rear bedroom so does not circulate through the entire cabin, leaving the lounge / kitchen area cold in winter. Also found the mattress very saggy and it sinks in the middle.

Regretting booking this campsite as there are much better options for a similar price in this area.
Recently stayed in our caravan. Slabbed sites are top class. Facility’s are spotless and well laid out.
Road noise a little bit off putting if on site near the road otherwise all good

TBH I’m confused why this Park is part of the Big4 group?
We generally rely on the standards the Big4 set, and this occasion we have been let down.
There are no cooking facilities in the camp kitchen other than mc oven, no pedal karts, no putt putt golf despite being advertised as available.
Access roads were muddy or if dry they’d be dusty and regardless they’re pot holed.
The park is generally run down and really it’s in the middle of nowhere, so you’ll need to drive to even get some takeaway or to go anywhere else you might like to go …..
I agree with others the amenities are clean but they’re very dated.
No boom gates, No security - so anyone can drive around at any time of the night or day, sussing out your camp and your stuff at their will !!
I strongly suggest that any prospective customer do reasonable research before booking here, as there are clearly better options in this area !!!
Easily our worst experience with Big4, needless to say we won’t be back!!!
Great friendly park.

Just a heads up to all families or people who have booked or planning to book that it has come to our attention that BIG4 Bays Port Stephens has kept a fire ban on site until the 1st of may despite their own discretion to change it in these unprecedentedly cold times this school holidays....despite there ont being a week and a half until May.... After speaking to them so far they have decided to keep their fire ban until 1st of May, this may impact families with small kids especially after the cold snap we've had with even snow already in the mountains, I know myself and other families and friends are thinking of cancelling out of concern as their are other surrounding accommodation that allow fire for warmth. When I spoke on the phone today the response I was given was curt and rude, however when I spoke to Lynn on the phone she was beautiful and tried to assist as much as she could despite it not being in her control. Whilst I understand this ban is outline in their T&C's I would think that since the ban is in their own discretion that they'd use their powers to accommodate for the safety and comfort of others by moving the ban forward by ONE week especially when paying over $500 to camp....now that it's dark by 5pm and we lose the warmth immediately this is a real concern as even eating dinner in the freezing cold isn't safe. It's now taken what is meant to be a fun and enjoyable holiday to something of high risk and concern.

It's now taken what is meant to be a fun and enjoyable holiday to something of high risk and concern.

We were so excited for this trip as it was the first time that we were taking our young child (under 5) to a BIG4 holiday park.

Good things:
- Pool and waterslide
- Shower water pressure in our caravan
- Location in regards to attractions around port stephens
- Park is medium sized and doesnt feel over crowded.
- Guests seemed to be nice and generally respectful, very family orientated crowd.
- Brazilian BBQ food truck on site -although they don't do kids portions so it was pretty $$$

Bad things
- We were allocated different accomodation then what we booked with a completely different floor plan. This was an issue because instead of having the kids room at the far end of the caravan, furthest from the entrance and all common spaces like living room, bathroom and kitchen. The kids beds were smack bang at the main entrance of the cabin and also in between all amenities in the caravan. This meant we had to creep around all night from 7:30pm onwards trying not to disturb their sleep.
- Bays did not advise that they were changing our accomodation although they did confirm the age of our child so some risk assessing and forethought to our situation would have been appreciated on their part or some discussion about it prior so we could explain that the accomodation they were allocating was unsuitable and why.
- There is no security on site at all. Anyone can come in and out of the property by car as there is no boom gate or cameras. Consequently due to no onsite security at night, there is constant drag racing for hours on the main road right outside EVERY night! We could smell burning rubber inside the caravan for over two hours our first night. By Sunday (Day 3 of our stay), one of the guests had become so fed up they went outside and told them to stop, which led to a 20min screaming argument with threats of violence being heard from inside our cabin. Eventually the police arrived and it stopped - That was the only somewhat quiet night we had out of 4nights.
- The main door to our caravan didnt close properly. There was also no way to secure it for safety from inside the cabin. This meant the only barrier between our small child and outside was a balcony style glass door with a latch that could be easily opened.  Due to this, we woke constantly worried if our child was still in their bed and had to check as we were concerned for their wellbeing and safety.
- The road and on-site traffic is loud in general, ear plugs would be nice. They are cheap and would show that management listens to feedback. Even if they made them available free at reception for those that required them.
- The parks facilities ie. games and activities are weathered and not well maintained. Alot of them unusable or not enjoyable. The park has so much potential so its such a shame as it wouldn't take that much money or time to maintain the equipment better.
- The tap water smells and tastes awful. I am not sure if this is a location thing or the caravan itself but we had to go and buy bottled water. It was that bad, I woke up with a sore throat after our first evening.
- There is no FAQ on the webite or provided on arrival with general quesitons about the property ie. Is the water safe to drink? Reception was only there for 6hrs a day due to it being a public holiday. Although they have an after hours number, the reception was so bad on site that making calls was near impossible. I tried when the drag racing had been going on for over two hours on our first night.

Overall we still had a good holiday but this was in no part to the holiday park itself, outside of the waterslide which was a big hit. After this experience we would definitely reconsider going to other holiday parks especially if we couldn't ensure the accomodation we booked was what we were going to receive. We would also check reviews before any decision as a lot of the issues we experienced seem to be long standing ones according to other reviews.
Own caravan sites large

We spent 3 days camping we had a great time pools was good kids loved the water slide  😁😁 spa was nice and relaxing
Amenities were clean
Area was very clean
very friendly staff
We will definitely be back next year

One of the worst caravan parks that I have been too.

Stayed there for Australia Day long weekend with family, we were put right next to the dump bin which was just squashed down every day and the smell got worse each time. No activities for the kids, the pool had no life guard and there was way to many people and kids almost drowning at times.

One of my family members had the shower head break off mid shower onto them and water going all over there belongings, he was only young and was terrified when he came out.

I myself went on the swing at the park with the kids and had my brand new shorts I purchased for the occasion rip as the swing was broken and rusty, I told reception straight away so no one else cut themselves the swing was taken down and staff at reception informed me they would call me on the Monday. I never heard back from the caravan park and each time I called staff were rude and hung up on my face when I explained my situation.. still haven’t heard anything back.

Don’t waste your time or money i wouldn’t go back even if it was free !!
Very friendly staff! Big expectation to Steve the maintenance guy made us feel at home! Will definitely be returning on our next visit! Thank you

Stayed for the long weekend first impressions NOT happy at all!!
1) NO SECURITY AT DOOR OR AROUND THE PARK
2) PEOPLE DRINKING ALCOHOL IN THE POOL  ,NO LIFE GUIDE OR SECURITY TO BE SEEN.
3) PEOPLE EXTREMELY LOUD THROUGHOUT THE NIGHT.
4) NO ENTERTAINMENT FOR KIDS PLAYGROUND NOT SAFE,
5) FACILITIES NOT CLEAN,
I FELT NOT SAFE USING THE FACILITIES AT NIGHT AS ANYONE CAN WALK IN  .......
The list can go on & on,  we will Not recommend the plc for anyone as it's not what it's advertised on there page,  we couldn't wait to leave.
Further to ur response :
1. I have personally came to the office to report the swing which deemed un safe that cut through my daughters short which also exposed rusty metal.
2. Regarding not reporting to the after hours contact number for security I have last call history on my mobile, which I am more than happy to provide that was made to you security number provided upon check in and located at the office door which no one responded too. I infact received a call the next day from reception when the matter was to late.
3. Alcohol- I understand with being Australia day and families enjoying them selfs however I feelt very uncomfortable with the minor adults drinking inside the swimming pool with children surround.

I am not happy with just a plain response like I mentioned am yet to receive a re ebursment as promised when I reported the incident on point 1.

Stayed there for 3 nights last weekend booked 3 power van sites with family, looks good on the big 4 website far from it had a powered caravan site no waste for van just running on the ground .no security.no boom gates anyone can drive in off the Street, dusty gravel driveways, no kids gocarts as advertised, goannas going thru camp sites.kids playground unsafe daughter cut her shorts and leg on rusty swing seat. Grandson had shower head fall off while showering.will never recommended anyone wasting the money at this place would of been better to camp on the side of the road ,won't be renewing my big 4 membership again will stick with NRMA parks.avoid I should of looked at the reviews before booking .

For the price I have paid for 5 nights I was expecting a lot more then what I got. There is no security anyone can walk into the park ,we are put right across what looks to be a old caravan holding bay amongst bins.
The pool is tiny and has a sewage smell the facilities let the place down it’s a neat little caravan place but very run down. Website is very convincing that this place is amazing which In fact this left my kids very disappointed expecting carts and mini golf as the website shows but none to be seen. after a huge stint of 4 other caravan parks this is a let down. It feels like a ghost town and you are also expected to wash all dishes and clean bins or will get charged ( mind you I do this anyway) but I find this very unhygienic as you don’t know if the person before you cleaned probably the fact there’s a sign in kitchen staying you will be charged for what is already a huge amount to pay! Every time you flush the toilet it’s sounds like a train is coming through so loud which wakes the kids up at night when we flush. For the price your expecting a 5star caravan park! We won’t be visiting again and in fact we are leaving earlier. For $2800 for 5 nights I’m gobsmacked. Pool area was closed without any notice. Our cabin was always bombarded by bin chickens and flies property as situated right at the bins. Found what looked to be a old chewing gum on the sheets

First initial thought of the park was average at best. No security over the peak holiday period (not an issue for us but some were concerned) No site had drainage / waste pipes under taps for caravans ect. All waste from each site was just left on the ground to dry up and stunk. 50% of parks roads are aggregate covered and very dangerous for children. Grass around the park besides office area was terribly long and attracted mozzies and flys. Water pressures in showers were terrible and very outdated facilities. I think the price we paid was very expensive considering the parks presentation and age. Sites were very short, most of the vans were hanging over the road 200/300mm. ||Overall: |Kids had fun and would be happy to stay again if the price reflected park. Maximum powered site charge should not be higher than $80 peak a day! ||PS: TAKE LOADS OF MOZZIE AND SAND FLEA REPELLENT!

Caravan space is a little small but functional, pool area is great but spa is not heated and there is a sewerage smell frequently around the area. Close to shops and beach and a few activities like camel rides, shark feeding, 4wd and mini golf

Rented a very small cabin for 1 night at a discounted $232.30!
Very clean rooms but the mattress was as hard as a rock with springs squeaking all night😫 plus no cooking facilities only a small outdoor bbq ,so cold porridge for us 😟 plus we we facing the office which had Christmas lights blinking on and off ALL NIGHT shining through every possible gap! Best part was the road traffic until late and we could hear the person adjoining our room coughing all night, talking or watching TV and constantly flushing the toilet or running water until way after 2am! Definitely not worth the exorbitant fee!
Wir waren für eine Nacht. Wir hatten ein Zelt, Toiletten veraltet und nicht sauber genug, schöne große Küche und Aufenthaltsraum. WiFi ohne Internet, kein Beach

We stayed on a powered site - the sites a very spacious, the amenities are dated but cleaned twice a day, a large kitchen, lots of space to walk (dog friendly too!)|There’s a lot for kids to do - bouncing pillow, pool, games etc ||I Highly recommend this Caravan Park

I booked last minute for the weekend and because the other parks were pretty much booked out I chose to stay here and was very happy with my stay. This park is also cheaper than the others and even more so with big 4 membership. The park isn’t next to the beach and the park is fairly basic however it still has 3 amenities blocks, camp kitchen, bbqs and a nice pool area with a pool suitable for small kids. There are also some nice cabins. The staff were great at check in. There was a bit of road noise but not too bad so long as you are not against the front fence. I think they use that section for when the rest of the park is full cos it was all  empty. The park welcomes dogs. There are lots of trees at the rear of the park for the doggies to sniff and explore. Not too far to drive to all the nice locations. Anna bay only minutes away and has a beautiful beach by the sand dunes that s also a doggy beach. Will definitely come back and stay here again

OMG, do not stay here. Management are rude and not helpful. They have built a function centre next to the cabins and have loud parties until late at night, including fireworks. We found a cockroach and ants in our cabins and a used Covid test under the fridge!! There is no security and no one to call at night with issues. Avoid this place!!
The staff were amazing and very hospitable. We camped down the back and it was very peaceful. Amazing fire pit too

My family and I just stayed here over the weekend in a pet friendly cabin with our 2 little dogs. We thought the layout of the cabin was lovely and felt very homey. Our favourite thing at the park was the jumping pillow so much fun for all of the family. The go cart buggy things were fun too and got lots of exercise in the process. The staff were all very lovely and the park was always kept clean and tidy. Overall we had a fantastic experience and will definitely be back for another weekend again one day soon.

My wife and I and our 2 dogs have stayed with our van many times over the last few years while visiting family, and have always been happy with the park. The facilities, laundry are all ok. The location has been convenient for us. Staff have always been pleasant and helpful.
The only negative is a bunch of ferals living over the road who seem to have a large collection of noisy cars and motorbikes which they use on the road and their property. My advice is to stay on a site or in a cabin as far from the road as possible.
Great place and very friendly staff

Such potential but you can have a fire at any site so the whole place is like a bushfire zone. Also has the ping pong table in the camp kitchen. Nightmare noise when u just want to cook and have a chat. Suggestion: designate one particular area that can have fires....not the whole park. And move the ping pong table to an outside location.

.....was such a nice spot.....but that killed it.
